issues.issue: Choice-4's 'confident-reassure' option is somewhat clearly the 'wrong' answer (Alex reacts coldly, calls it out directly), making it feel less like a genuine trade-off and more like an obvious trap.
issues.impact: Reduces the feeling of a genuine dilemma; players optimizing for 'good outcomes' will quickly learn to avoid this option rather than feel torn.
issues.fix: Give confident-reassure a plausible short-term upside (e.g., Alex almost smiles before pulling back) so all three options feel like reasonable choices rather than one clearly punished path.

issues.issue: Several 1-scene, single-beat scenes (cafeteria-buzz, guest-list-tease, priya-pushback) are very short and dialogue-dense back-to-back, creating a rapid string of similar cafeteria conversations before the first real change of location.
issues.impact: Early pacing can feel repetitive/samey (same location, same characters, similar beat) before variety kicks in with hallway/classroom scenes.
issues.fix: Vary staging slightly (e.g., a brief non-dialogue beat, a locker-hall transition) or compress two of the three opening scenes into one to accelerate to the first real choice.

issues.issue: Endings are well-differentiated in dialogue but structurally converge quickly (three endings, four-to-six lines each) — the emotional payoff difference is more tonal than mechanical.
issues.impact: Replay curiosity may be satisfied after 2 playthroughs since the endings are short and structurally similar in length/format.
issues.fix: Add a small unique visual/interactive beat or callback detail per ending (e.g., a specific object, a line referencing a very early choice) to make each feel more distinct on replay.

issues.issue: No explicit onboarding for how INTERNAL lines / branchDialogue work; players relying on trait feedback won't see it directly since traits are backend-only in this text.
issues.impact: New players may not realize their earlier choices are being tracked into named 'paths' (Advocate, Repair, etc.) since this is invisible in dialogue except through subtle branchDialogue callbacks.
issues.fix: Consider a very subtle end-of-episode signal (not heavy-handed) confirming which 'kind of choices' the player tended to make, reinforcing the replay hook.

strengths: Excellent choice-consequence tracking: branchDialogue explicitly references earlier micro-choices (e.g., whether Sol laughed at the joke) inside the endings, giving strong felt impact
Genuine dilemmas throughout — Priya's jabs are funny/sharp rather than cartoonishly villainous, so 'easy' choices are seductive rather than obviously wrong
Strong escalation structure: low-stakes cafeteria banter → semi-private hallway vulnerability → public high-stakes party test mirrors natural tension build
Good variety of choice types: social confrontation, private repair, ambiguous 'wait and see' choices, and final loyalty test at climax
Three distinct, well-earned endings with a repair path that rewards recovery from an early misstep rather than permanently locking players out of a good outcome
Convergence design (multiple entry scenes funneling into the same 3 endings via choice-6a/6b/choice-7) is efficient without feeling repetitive because setup dialogue differs per path
recommendations: Add a small unique beat/detail to each ending to boost replay distinctiveness
Consider softening the 'wrong answer' feel of the confident-reassure option in choice-4
Introduce slight scene/location variety earlier to prevent the first four scenes feeling repetitive
Add a subtle mid-episode moment (e.g., a text notification, a small choice with Ms. Chen) to break up the lunch/hallway rhythm before the party climax

summary.verdict: A well-constructed, emotionally resonant branching episode with strong choice-consequence linkage and a satisfying convergent structure. The social dilemmas feel genuine rather than moralized, and the repair-path design cleverly avoids punishing players permanently for an early misstep. Minor pacing repetitiveness in the opening and slightly transparent 'correct answer' framing in one choice are the only real blemishes.
keyIssues: Opening three scenes are same-location/same-beat and slightly repetitive
One choice option (confident-reassure) reads as an obvious misstep rather than a genuine trade-off
Endings, while well-written, are structurally short and similar in shape
topStrengths: Branch-aware ending dialogue that reflects the player's specific path
Sharp, non-cartoonish antagonist dynamics (Priya) that make 'easy' choices genuinely tempting
Strong tension-release rhythm building to the party climax
niceToHave: Vary early scene staging/location for pacing variety
Add unique callback details per ending for replay distinctiveness
Rebalance confident-reassure option in choice-4 to feel less punitive
